creator: sghctoma@gmail.com
text:
I am running FreeBSD 8 on a Lenovo ThinkPad x200s laptop. This model has a TrackPoint as a pointing device which is working fine as a Generic PS/2 Mouse, but I did not find a way to properly customize it (I could not achieve the sensitivity and smoothness I am accostumed to, not to mention Press to Select options).

Fix: In the attached patch I introduced some sysctl MIBs under hw.psm.trackpoint, and a tunable (hw.psm.trackpoint_support) to turn on/off TrackPoint support (similarly to hw.psm.synaptics.* and hw.psm.synaptics_support). I got information about the device from the document "TrackPoint System Version 4.0 Engineering Specification" which is available at http://wwwcssrv.almaden.ibm.com/trackpoint/files/ykt3eext.pdf

Patch attached with submission follows:
How-To-Repeat: Just use FreeBSD on any TrackPoint-equipped ThinkPad.
